summary: Removed the item-local itHit struct and replaced item hitbox uses with the shared HitCapsule layout. The PR rationale was that item collision code passed &Item->x5D4 into functions expecting HitCapsule, and known regions of itHit and HitCapsule were identical. Item::x5D4_hitboxes became an array of small wrappers containing HitCapsule hit plus the trailing x138 field, function prototypes were updated from itHit* to HitCapsule*, and field accesses were translated from copied itHit names to HitCapsule names such as hit.state and hit.damage.
what_changed: Deleted struct itHit from src/melee/it/types.h, removing a duplicate item-hitbox layout with copied-looking named members.
Removed typedef forward declarations for itHit and itHurt from src/melee/it/forward.h.
Changed it_80272460 from taking itHit* to taking HitCapsule* in src/melee/it/it_266F.h.
Changed it_8026B1D4 from taking itHit* to taking HitCapsule* in both declaration and definition.
Updated it_8026B1D4 to read HitCapsule::damage instead of itHit::xC_damage_staled.
Changed Item::x5D4_hitboxes from itHit x5D4_hitboxes[4] to an anonymous wrapper array containing HitCapsule hit and s32 x138.
Updated item.c hitbox iteration to check hit.state against HitCapsule_Disabled instead of using x0_toggle, read hit.damage instead of xC_damage_staled, and pass &x5D4_hitboxes[i].hit to it_80272460.
Added a comment marking the 0x134 offset in struct HitCapsule near the owner/hit_grabbed_victim_only union.
smart_moves: Used call-site evidence to justify the type merge: item collision code was already passing Item::x5D4 storage to APIs that conceptually expected HitCapsule.
Preserved the full item hitbox storage size by wrapping HitCapsule with a trailing s32 x138 rather than blindly replacing it with a bare HitCapsule array.
Translated semantic fields to the existing shared HitCapsule names instead of keeping duplicate or m-ex-derived itHit field names.
Narrowed API signatures to the shared collision type, reducing future divergence between item hitboxes and general hit capsules.
decomp_lessons: When two structs appear layout-compatible, verify through real call sites and known identical regions before merging them.
Prefer one authoritative shared collision struct over parallel item-specific copies when the binary usage supports it.
If a removed struct has extra trailing data relative to the replacement type, model that explicitly with a wrapper so offsets remain preserved.
Copied external struct/member names should be treated as provisional; remove or rename them when the project can independently verify the layout and semantics.
Field renames during struct deduplication should use established semantic names from the retained type, e.g. HitCapsule::damage and HitCapsule::state.
naming_conventions: Use HitCapsule for item hitbox data when item collision storage matches the shared hit capsule layout.
Use the nested member name hit for the HitCapsule portion of Item::x5D4_hitboxes wrapper entries.
Use HitCapsule_Disabled for disabled hitbox state checks instead of item-local boolean toggle names.
Prefer established HitCapsule member names such as damage and state over itHit-style offset names like xC_damage_staled and x0_toggle.
Keep unknown trailing storage named by offset, as with x138, when its purpose is not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Struct replacement was done by preserving layout-sensitive offsets: Item::x5D4_hitboxes still stores four entries, each containing a HitCapsule plus trailing x138.
Pointer adjustments were made explicitly at use sites: callers now pass &temp_item->x5D4_hitboxes[i].hit when a HitCapsule* is required.
Matching-friendly field substitutions mapped old offsets to equivalent HitCapsule fields, notably xC_damage_staled to damage and x0_toggle to hit.state != HitCapsule_Disabled.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: The removed itHit struct and named members appeared to have been copied from m-ex, and the author stated that removing and independently verifying them was in line with the project's current approach.

follow_up_queries: Audit remaining item collision structs for duplicated HitCapsule or HurtCapsule layouts.
Search for remaining references to m-ex-derived field names in item collision code.
Investigate the purpose of the trailing x138 field in Item::x5D4_hitboxes wrapper entries.
Verify whether removed itHurt forward declaration had no remaining users or should map to HurtCapsule in a similar cleanup.
